---
title: "Opening a pull request"
description: "Branch, changelog, and the checklist before requesting a review."
order: 50
---

Changes reach OpenPipeline through pull requests against the **`main`** branch of
the package repository (see
[Versioning and releases](project-structure.qmd#sec-versioning)).

## The flow

1. Work on a branch in your fork (see [Getting started](getting-started.qmd)).
2. Make your change and [test it locally](running-tests.qmd).
3. Add an entry to the repository's `CHANGELOG.md` describing the change.
4. Push to your fork and open a pull request against `main`.

## Before requesting a review

The pull-request template asks you to confirm:

- you have done a **self-review** of your changes;
- the change is **described in `CHANGELOG.md`**;
- the PR **links its issue** (`Closes #<number>`);
- you have **categorized** the change — breaking change, new functionality, major
  change, minor change, documentation, or bug fix;
- the **CI tests pass** (they run automatically on the PR).

A maintainer reviews the pull request and, once approved, merges it into `main`.
From there [Viash Hub builds and publishes](project-structure.qmd#sec-versioning)
the package with the next release.
